rationale: The current best run already improved the endgame by snapping weights through the final int8 roundtrip. This change attacks the same objective more directly by reducing quantization error itself: large 2D tensors now use per-row grouped scales across columns instead of a single scale for the whole row. That should preserve more post-training signal after the exact int8+zlib roundtrip, while the extra scale metadata stays well within the current 16MB artifact headroom. The late quant-aware roundtrips automatically become more faithful because they now use the stronger grouped quantizer too.
title: Row-Group Int8 Quantization Alignment
